void __thiscall CClientSocket::ProcessPacket(CClientSocket *this, CInPacket *a2)
{
  int op;
  op = CInPacket::Decode2(a2);
  switch ( op )
  {
    case 0x11u:
      CLogin::OnFoo(this, a2);
      break;
    case 0x12u:
    case 0x13u:
      CLogin::OnBarBaz(this, a2);
      break;
    case 200u:
      CLogin::OnCheckPasswordResult(this, a2);
      break;
    case 0x20u:
      sub_5E1230(this, a2);
      break;
    case 0x21u:
      CLogin::OnAccountInfoResult(this, a2);
      break;
    case 0x22u:
      CLogin::OnWorldInformation(this, a2);
      break;
    // ---- noise-hardening cases below (task-085 robustness probe) ----
    case 0x30u:
      // COutPacket ctor noise: must not bind as handler
      COutPacket::COutPacket(&v9, 6);
      // alloca noise line
      alloca(v5);
      // operator new noise: v4 = operator new(0x44u)
      v4 = operator new(0x44u);
      // TSingleton::GetInstance noise (regex breaks at '<', captures bare class name)
      TSingleton::GetInstance();
      // vtable call noise: (*(void(**)(...))(...)) — captures 'void'
      (*(void(**)(CClientSocket *, CInPacket *))(*((_DWORD *)v3)))(v3, a2);
      // real handler comes after all the noise
      CLogin::OnSelectWorld(this, a2);
      break;
    case 0x31u:
      // goto-tail: label must not leak into the next case
      CLogin::OnExtraWork(this, a2);
      goto LABEL_END;
    case 0x32u:
      // This case must bind its own handler, not bleed from goto case above
      CLogin::OnCharList(this, a2);
      break;
    case 0x40u:
      // nested switch inside a case body — inner labels must not become top-level opcodes
      {
        int sub_op;
        sub_op = CInPacket::Decode1(a2);
        switch ( sub_op )
        {
          case 0x01u:
            CLogin::OnSubOpA(this, a2);
            break;
          case 0x02u:
            CLogin::OnSubOpB(this, a2);
            break;
        }
      }
      CLogin::OnMigrateIn(this, a2);
      break;
    default:
      break;
  }
LABEL_END:
  return;
}
